topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at the conservation, regulation, and management of wildlife species and their habitats in the United States.

Document 1: Invasive Fish and Wildlife Prevention Act of 2012
Requires FWS to determine whether the proposal should be approved within 180 days.
Document 2: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973
Determination of endangered or threatened species.
Document 3: Farmers Protection Act of 2012
Amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to permit the taking of any migratory game bird.
Document 4: Amends the National Wildlife Refuge System Administration Act of 1966
Designate a portion of the Arctic National Wildlife Refuge as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Document 5: Migratory Bird Treaty Amendment Act of 2011
Allows an agricultural producer to take migratory game birds by the aid of baiting.
Document 6: Big Cats and Public Safety Protection Act
Prohibit any person from importing, exporting, transporting, selling, receiving, acquiring, or purchasing any prohibited wildlife species.

topic.name: Sustainable Energy Solutions
topic.description: The documents focus on various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting sustainable energy practices and reducing environmental impact.

doc1: impact of solar power on energy consumption
doc2: case studies on sustainable energy implementation

topic.name: sustainable energy solutions
topic.description: This topic encompasses various strategies and technologies aimed at promoting renewable energy sources and reducing environmental impact.

doc1: renewable energy sources such as solar and wind
doc2: policy frameworks for sustainable energy adoption

topic.name: Medicare and Health Policy Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments related to Medicare and health policy, addressing funding, program modifications, and quality measures.

Document 1: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
defund multi-stakeholder groups for input into health care quality measures
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
repeal administrative penalties for eligible professionals who are not successful electronic prescribers
Document 3: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
requires alignment of updates for ambulatory surgical center services
Document 4: Amends title V (Maternal and Child Health Services) of the Social Security Act
changes the funding mechanism for personal responsibility education programs
Document 5: Amends title V (Maternal and Child Health Services) of the Social Security Act
eliminate the abstinence-only education program
Document 6: Amends part D (Child Support and Establishment of Paternity) of title IV of the Social Security Act
expand the authority of the Secretary of the Treasury to access the National Directory of New Hires
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Defense and the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to jointly establish the Advisory Board on Military Exposures
authorize health care benefits recommended by the Board

topic.name: Homebuyer and Mortgage Tax Incentives
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at providing tax benefits and incentives for homebuyers and homeowners regarding mortgage payments and related expenses.

Document 1: Amends the Credit Repair Organizations Act to exempt from its coverage any consumer reporting agency...
Preempts state law and regulations concerning a credit repair organization.
Document 2: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a refundable tax credit equal to 50% of the excess home mortgage principal payments made by a taxpayer during the year.
The credit is limited to $500 per year and to taxpayers who have not received the credit for any of the 10 preceding years.
Document 3: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to provide for tax-preferred savings accounts for first-time homebuyers.
Distributions from the account that are used to pay the qualified principal residence purchase expenditures of the designated beneficiary are excluded from gross income.
Document 4: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through December 31, 2017, the tax deduction for mortgage insurance premiums treated as interest.
Document 5: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through December 31, 2018, the tax deduction for mortgage insurance premiums treated as interest.
Document 6: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase by 25% the allowable tax deduction for mortgage interest on a principal residence until 2014.
Waive the requirement to repay credit amounts under the first-time homebuyer tax credit for residences purchased on or after April 9, 2008.
Document 7: This bill amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend the tax deduction for new business expenditures to organizational expenditures.
Increases the maximum deduction amount for start-up and organizational expenditures from $5,000 to $20,000.

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States concerning the temporary suspension of duties on various specific materials and products.

Document 1: extends the temporary suspension of duty on certain untwisted filament yarns.
Document 2: extends the temporary suspension of duty on certain synthetic filament yarns.
Document 3: extends the temporary suspension of duty on aqueous dispersions of poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene) poly(styrenesulfonate).
Document 4: extends the temporary suspension of duty on aqueous catalytic preparations based on iron (III) toluenesulfonate.
Document 5: extends temporarily the suspension of duty on 3 Chloro 4 Methyl Aniline.
Document 6: extends the temporary suspension of duty on noils of camel hair.
Document 7: extends temporarily the reduction of duty on certain men's footwear.

topic.name: U.S. Military and Defense Policies
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative measures related to the management, operations, and welfare of military personnel and defense strategies in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits the Attorney General from initiating a custodial interrogation of foreign persons detained by the United States.
Requires consultation with intelligence officials before taking action against foreign persons.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Defense to establish a board of review for mental health determinations of separated servicemembers.
Defines covered individuals as members and former members of the Armed Forces separated due to mental conditions.
Document 3: Prohibits operations involving armed unmanned aerial vehicles by federal agencies other than the Department of Defense.
Authorizes only uniformed members of the Armed Forces to operate armed UAVs.
Document 4: Requires loans to be repaid to the U.S. government from the Iraq Security Forces Fund.
Amends the National Defense Authorization Act to withhold funds until certification from the Secretary of Defense.
Document 5: Directs the Department of Defense to submit a plan to enhance security for military personnel at installations.
Requires implementation of the plan within 90 days.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of the Air Force to maintain the current leadership rank and capabilities at Eielson Air Force Base.
Prohibits changes until completion of an environmental impact statement.
Document 7: Requires transfer of members receiving outpatient care at Walter Reed to the Secretary of Veterans Affairs.
Directs joint provision of information on outpatient care services available through the VA.

topic.name: Land Management and Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at the management, conservation, and transfer of public lands for ecological and recreational purposes.

Document 1: Grants the city of Mesquite, Nevada, the exclusive right to purchase certain parcels of public land
Requires proceeds from certain sales to be used for the implementation of the Virgin River Multispecies Habitat Conservation Plan.
Document 2: Establishes the Preservation Area for the protection, conservation, and enhancement of habitat for the lesser prairie chicken.
Requires the Secretary to develop a plan for the long-range protection and management of the Preservation Area.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of the Interior to convey specified federal lands and interests to the Ketchikan Gateway Borough in Alaska.
Requires the Connell Lake conveyance area to be managed in accordance with the terms of the Recreation and Public Purposes Act.
Document 4: Transfers administrative jurisdiction over the Joliet Training Area from the Secretary of the Army to the Secretary of Agriculture.
Requires the conveyed lands to only be used for parks and recreation.
Document 5: Sets forth requirements concerning the maintenance of viable populations of existing native and desired non-native species within each planning area.
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of the Interior to adopt and implement a comprehensive monitoring program for determining the status and trends of wildlife populations.
Document 6: Permits Clark County, Nevada, to convey approximately 51 acres of land to the Nevada Division of State Lands for use by the Nevada National Guard.
Provides for the reversion of such land to Clark County for management in accordance with the Southern Nevada Public Land Management Act of 1998.
Document 7: Authorizes the Utah National Parks Council of the Boy Scouts of America to convey to Brian Head Resort certain public land in Utah.
For the purpose of operating a camp in exchange for conveyance by Brian Head Resort of certain of its private land to the Boy Scouts.

topic.name: Veterans' Benefits and Support
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation and policies related to the benefits, support, and services provided to veterans and their families.

Document 1: Amends veterans' benefit provisions to provide for the payment of dependency and indemnity compensation to the survivors of veterans rated totally disabled at the time of death who were former prisoners of war.
Provides for the survivors of such veterans who died after September 30, 1999.
Document 2: Establishes in the Department of Defense (DOD) an Office of the Victims' Advocate to facilitate access to services for victims of domestic or family violence, sexual assault, and stalking in the military.
Directs the Secretary of Defense to require DOD policies for victim assistance.
Document 3: Defines 'combat with the enemy' for purposes of proof of service-connection for veterans' disability compensation.
Service on active duty in a theater of combat operations during a period of war.

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Reform
topic.description: Legislative amendments and acts related to the U.S. immigration system and citizenship policies.

Document 1: Birthright Citizenship Act of 2011 - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to consider a person born in the United States 'subject to the jurisdiction' of the United States for citizenship at birth purposes.
person is born in the United States of parents, one of whom is: (1) a U.S. citizen or national.
Document 2: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996 to require federal contractors and subcontractors to participate in the E-Verify Program.
Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to ensure that the Program is applied to all of the contractor's or subcontractor's employees.
Document 3: Stopping Trained in America Ph.D.s From Leaving the Economy Act of 2011 or the STAPLE Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to exempt from: (1) direct numerical limitations aliens who have earned a Ph.D. degree in science, technology, engineering, or mathematics (STEM).
Document 4: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to allow states to be compensated for incarcerating undocumented aliens who are charged with, or convicted of, a felony or two or more misdemeanors.
Document 5: Nuclear Family Priority Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to replace existing family-sponsored immigrant categories with a single preference allocation for spouses and children of permanent resident aliens.
Document 6: Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to eliminate the diversity immigrant program and provide an equal number of annual immigrant visas (55,000) to certain employment-based aliens.
Document 7: Liberian Refugee Immigration Fairness Act of 2011 - Adjusts to permanent resident status a qualifying Liberian national who: (1) has been continuously present in the United States.

topic.name: Substance Abuse Legislation
topic.description: Legislation addressing various aspects of substance abuse, including opioids, marijuana, and synthetic drugs.

Document 1: Opioid Abuse Crisis Act of 2016
amends the Public Health Service Act to require the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to provide support for activities related to opioid abuse
Document 2: Marijuana Advertising in Legal States Act of 2016 or the MAILS Act
modifies the application of a provision that limits advertisements with respect to certain controlled substances
Document 3: Stop Meth Labs and Enhance Patient Access Act of 2015
amends the Controlled Substances Act to revise the standard used by the Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA)
Document 4: Stop the Overdose Problem Already Becoming a Universal Substance Epidemic Act of 2015 or the STOP ABUSE Act of 2015

requires the Department of Health and Human Services to convene an interagency task force to develop, solicit input on, disseminate information about, and study implementation of best practices for pain management and prescription of pain medication
Document 5: Dangerous Synthetic Drug Control Act of 2016
amends the Controlled Substances Act to add certain synthetic opioids, hallucinogens, and cannabinoids to schedule I

topic.name: Transportation Regulations and Consumer Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses various regulations and consumer rights related to air and rail transportation, focusing on service quality, safety, and compliance requirements for carriers and operators.

Document 1: air carrier slots at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport
nonstop air transportation service
Document 2: eliminates certain grounds for waiving Buy American requirements
federal funding for certain air navigation, highway, and rail activities
Document 3: disclose the nature and source of flight delays and cancellations
air carriers that provide foreign air transportation
Document 4: positive train control systems implementation requirements
intercity or commuter rail passenger transportation
Document 5: Airline Passenger Bill of Rights Act of 2009
passengers on a departure- or arrival-delayed grounded aircraft
Document 6: Airline Passenger Bill of Rights Act of 2011
civil penalty against air carriers and airport operators
Document 7: timely and fair process for individuals who believe they were delayed
Office of Appeals and Redress within DHS

topic.name: Labor and Employment Law
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts and amendments related to the rights and protections of employees in the workplace.

Document 1: Amends federal arbitration law to exclude from the definition of 'commerce' all contracts of employment.
Defines 'employment dispute' as a dispute between an employer and employee arising out of the employer-employee relationship.
Document 2: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to prohibit the National Labor Relations Board from requiring an employer to provide employee contact information.
Document 3: Prohibits the Secretary of Homeland Security from approving an employer petition for any visa authorizing employment unless certain conditions are met.
States that visas approved during the most recent 12-month period shall expire if a mass layoff notice is provided.
Document 4: Requires an employer to give 90-day written notice before ordering a plant closing or mass layoff.
Makes an employer liable for failing to provide notice to employees.
Document 5: Revises requirements for miners' claims for pneumoconiosis (black lung) benefits.
Establishes standards of medical evidence that establish a presumption that a miner is totally disabled by black lung.
Document 6: Allows a pilot who has attained 60 years of age to serve as a passenger airline pilot until the age of 65 years old under certain conditions.
Document 7: Amends the Civil Rights Act of 1964 to declare that an unlawful employment practice occurs when a discriminatory compensation decision is adopted.
Allows liability to accrue for up to two years preceding the filing of the charge.

topic.name: Naming Postal Facilities
topic.description: The documents discuss measures to designate various United States Postal Service facilities in honor of individuals and veterans.

Document 1: Designates the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) medical center in Waco, Texas, as the 'Doris Miller Department of Veterans Affairs Medical Center.'
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 4000 Leap Road in Hilliard, Ohio, as the 'Sergeant Shawn T. Hannon and Master Sergeant Jeffrey J. Rieck and Veterans Memorial Post Office Building.'
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 18640 NW 2nd Avenue in Miami, Florida, as the 'Father Richard Marquess-Barry Post Office Building.'
Document 4: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 218-10 Merrick Boulevard in Springfield Gardens, New York, as the 'Cynthia Jenkins Post Office Building.'
Document 6: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 90 Vermilyea Avenue, in New York, New York as the 'Corporal Juan Mariel Alcantara Post Office Building.'

topic.name: Small Business Regulation and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing support and reducing regulatory burdens for small businesses in the United States.

Document 1: encourage establishment of comprehensive safety and health management systems
establish a voluntary protection program to encourage excellence
Document 2: revises the regulatory process with respect to small entities
expands judicial review of agency rulemaking
Document 3: excludes Alaska Native Corporations from the definition of 'Indian tribe'
makes members of socially and economically disadvantaged ANCs or ANVs eligible for such loans
Document 4: increase the maximum amount of outstanding leverage by the Small Business Administration
to two or more commonly controlled small business investment companies
Document 5: authorize the Secretary of Labor to award competitive grants to local boards
eliminate gaps between the workforce skills available and the workforce skills needed
Document 6: directs the SEC to modify its regulations under the Sarbanes-Oxley Act
exempt a non-accelerated filer from certain reporting requirements

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to the operations, employee rights, and financial management of the U.S. Postal Service.

Document 1: Extends federal employee protections against adverse personnel actions
right to appeal such actions to the Merit Systems Protection Board
Document 2: Sets forth rules for the recalculation of annuities for employees of the U.S. Postal Service
Eliminates the requirement for pre-funding of the Postal Service Retiree Health Benefits Fund
Document 3: Directs the Postmaster General to issue a commemorative postage stamp in honor of Ebenezer D. Bassett
Document 4: Prohibits mail processing facilities from being closed or consolidated prior to December 31, 2015
Document 5: policy of the U.S. Postal Service to ensure reasonable and sustainable workloads and schedules for supervisory and management employees
Document 6: removes the authority of the U.S. Postal Service to issue semipostal stamps except as provided by an Act of Congress
Document 7: makes it a crime to use the mail to knowingly publish personally identifiable information of another person

topic.name: Corporate Campaign Finance Regulation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at regulating corporate contributions and expenditures in elections to prevent foreign influence and ensure shareholder approval.

Document 1: prohibit a corporation from making any independent expenditure without obtaining the prior approval of a majority of its shareholders
subjects officers and directors of a corporation to personal liability for civil money penalties
Document 2: apply the ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nationals to domestic corporations
percentage of outstanding shares controlled directly or indirectly by foreign principals is equal to or greater than 50%
Document 3: apply the ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nationals to domestic corporations whose shareholders include one or more foreign principals
Document 4: apply the ban on contributions and expenditures by foreign nations to domestic corporations
one or more foreign principals serves on the board of directors
Document 5: prohibit corporations from making any expenditure if they employ or retain a registered lobbyist
Document 6: apply the prohibition against the conversion to personal use of contributions accepted by candidates also to contributions accepted by political committees

topic.name: Child Welfare and Parental Rights
topic.description: Legislation focused on the protection of minors in relation to parental rights, particularly concerning abortion and educational privacy.

Document 1: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
deems such transporting to be a de facto abridgment of the right of a parent
Document 2: Prohibits a federal official from expending federal funds for any foreign or domestic population control
family planning activity (including any abortion procedure)
Document 3: prohibit funding of educational agencies or institutions that allow third parties to access student data
requires parental consent before authorized representatives may access records
Document 4: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion

makes it an affirmative defense to a prosecution or civil action under this Act that a defendant reasonably believed that before the minor obtained the abortion, the required parental consent or notification took place
Document 5: prohibit transporting a minor child across a state line to obtain an abortion
requires such physician to give 24-hour actual or constructive notice to a parent of the minor seeking an abortion
Document 6: revise the requirements for a state plan to administer a child care development block grant
minimum health and safety training appropriate to a child care provider mandatory under state or local law

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Education
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to the management, disposal, and educational use of federal lands in the United States.

Document 1: Catastrophic Wildfire Community Protection Act directs projects on National Forest System lands and public lands to protect at-risk communities.
Requires environmental assessments for proposed projects.
Document 2: Action Plan for Public Lands and Education Act of 2011 makes grants of land to states for public education support.
Requires proceeds from land sales to be used only for education.
Document 3: Disposal of Excess Federal Lands Act directs the Secretary of the Interior to offer federal lands for competitive sale.
Identifies certain federal lands in multiple states for disposal.
Document 4: Action Plan for Public Lands and Education Act of 2011 makes grants of land to states for public education support.
Requires proceeds from land sales to be used only for education.
Document 5: Powell Shooting Range Land Conveyance Act directs the Secretary of the Interior to convey land for public use.
Requires the District to pay administrative costs associated with the land conveyance.
Document 6: Grand Canyon Watersheds Protection Act withdraws federal land from entry and disposal under public land laws.
Subjects withdrawal to valid, existing rights.

topic.name: Firearms Legislation and Rights
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to the possession, carrying, and regulation of firearms on federal lands and across state lines.

Document 1: Prohibits any federal regulation from restricting any individual from possessing or carrying a firearm in a unit of the National Park System.
Document 2: Prohibits any federal regulation from restricting any individual from possessing or carrying a firearm or ammunition in federal lands.
Sets forth provisions regarding hunting activities on federal lands.
Document 3: Amends the federal criminal code to provide for reciprocity for carrying concealed firearms in different states.
Document 4: Prohibits transferring firearms from business inventory into a personal collection after license revocation.
Document 5: Provides for reciprocity for carrying certain concealed firearms in different states.

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Compliance
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at enhancing financial regulation, transparency, and compliance within the financial sector.

Document 1: Financial Transparency Act of 2015 directs the Office of Financial Research to adopt data standards for financial data.
The SEC must publish all public information as open data.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Credit Union Act regarding insured amounts payable in connection with a bankrupt state-chartered credit union.
Defines 'IOLTA' as a system in which lawyers place certain client funds in interest-bearing accounts.
Document 3: Amends the Comprehensive Iran Sanctions, Accountability, and Divestment Act of 2010 to direct the Secretary of the Treasury to determine affiliations with Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps.
Specifies prohibitions on U.S. correspondent accounts of foreign financial institutions.
Document 4: Regulatory Easement for Lending Institutions that Enable a Vibrant Economy Act of 2014 directs the Federal Reserve to revise policies for small bank holding companies.
Amends the Truth in Lending Act to define 'rural' for residential mortgage loan applications.
Document 5: Iranian Energy Sector and Proliferation Sanctions Act directs the President to impose sanctions on transactions with Iranian entities involved in petroleum activities.
Exempts certain crude oil purchases from sanctions under specified conditions.
Document 6: Allows the EPA to designate air quality empowerment zones based on specific air pollution and health criteria.
Authorizes grants for areas designated as air and health quality empowerment zones.
Document 7: Flood Insurance Rate Increase Suspension Act bars increases in flood insurance premium rates for a 12-month period.
Establishes a framework for yearly increases in premium rates after the suspension.

topic.name: Employment and Health Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses legislation aimed at protecting workers' rights, health, and safety in the workplace.

Document 1: ensure that American workers are allowed to follow, without financial harm, employer and public health authority recommendations to stay home when they have symptoms of a contagious disease
Requires an employer to provide paid sick leave to an employee for each workday, or portion of workday, that the employer directs the employee to leave work
Document 4: Amends the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 to deny jurisdiction to a state or federal court in a civil action a plaintiff commences for remedies for disability discrimination
the plaintiff notified the defendant in writing of the alleged violations prior to filing the complaint
Document 7: amends the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967 to specify that an unlawful employment practice is established when the complaining party demonstrates that age or participation in investigations, proceedings, or litigation under such Act was a motivating factor
permits a complaining party to rely on any type or form of admissible evidence

topic.name: Federal Accountability and Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing transparency, accountability, and oversight of federal expenditures and financial regulations.

Document 1: Amends the Buy American Act to require each federal department to submit a report that contains an evaluation of contractor compliance.
Requires the report to be publicly available.
Document 2: Requires the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to establish a publicly available website for tracking expenditures.
Document 4: Establishes an independent Financial Stability Council to review and approve regulations issued by federal financial regulators.
Subjects violations of this Act to civil penalties.
Document 5: Directs the President to modify and update the recovery.gov website to ensure eligibility for funds under the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act.

topic.name: Biofuels and Renewable Energy Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation and initiatives aimed at promoting the research, development, and infrastructure for biofuels and other renewable energy sources in the United States.

Document 1: establish a technology transfer center to disseminate information on research, development, and commercial application of technologies related to specified biofuels
requires the Secretary to report to Congress on challenges inherent in increasing to 2.5% the biodiesel proportion of diesel fuel sold in the United States.
Document 2: establish an infrastructure development grants program to assist motor fuel dealers to implement motor fuel storage and dispensing infrastructure for renewable fuel
instructs the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to study and report to Congress on domestic utilization of ethanol blended gasoline with levels of ethanol greater than 10%.
Document 3: revise the definition of 'renewable biomass' to include biomass removed from certain federal lands
in connection with an authorized hazardous fuel reduction project.
Document 4: seek to establish a regional-based ministerial forum for the cooperation of Western Hemisphere on energy issues
to permit participating countries to cooperatively address broad challenges posed to the energy supply of the Western Hemisphere.
Document 5: declare a national goal to achieve 20% of total electrical energy production in the United States from geothermal resources by 2030
instructs the Secretary of Energy to implement a geothermal research and development program to achieve such goals.

topic.name: Aquatic Invasive Species Management
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at preventing and managing the transfer of aquatic invasive species in the Great Lakes and related waterways.

1: Requires the Chief of Engineers...to take actions to prevent the transfer of aquatic invasive species, with a focus on Asian carp species.
Directs the EPA Administrator...to carry out engineering and construction of flood mitigation and water quality measures...related to permanent prevention of the transfer of aquatic nuisance species.
2: Requires the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to create an online database of research and information on the causes and corrective actions being taken with regard to algal blooms in the Great Lakes.
Requires NOAA to include relevant chemical, physical, and biological data that have been collected by...federal agency, state, or local government in the United States or Canada.
3: Amends the federal criminal code to prohibit the importation into or shipment within the United States of the species of mussels of the genus Dreissena.
Makes this Act inapplicable to...the possession or conveyance of water supplies containing such species by a public water system operator.
5: Requires the Chief of Engineers...to take actions to prevent the transfer of aquatic invasive species, with a focus on Asian carp species.
Directs the EPA Administrator...to carry out engineering and construction of flood mitigation and water quality measures...related to permanent prevention of the transfer of aquatic nuisance species.
